description: Positive cases: prompts where the user wants to dispose of a single feature branch (merge, MR, keep, or discard). Negative cases: multi-service orchestration, cleanup/archive, review, plain test runs.

positive_cases.prompt: Merge this branch into the baseline.
Finish this branch — I'm done with it.
I'm done with this feature branch for order-service.
This worktree is ready. Tests pass and the code looks good. Let's wrap it up.
Discard this branch — I went the wrong direction.
positive_cases.reason_for_excluding: TRICK CASE: user explicitly names the discard disposition. Should still trigger so the skill confirms before deleting.

positive_cases.prompt: Keep this branch for now but wrap up what you're doing.
Push this branch and open a GitLab MR targeting the iteration baseline.
positive_cases.reason_for_excluding: TRICK CASE: user asks for an MR. The skill should still fire — it runs the build first before offering GitLab MR as a disposition. A bare 'create an MR' without finishing context is different.

negative_cases.prompt: Merge all the service branches for PROJ-1234 into the baseline.
negative_cases.reason_for_excluding: Multi-service branch orchestration is multi-branch-merge, not finishing-a-feature-branch.

negative_cases.prompt: Clean up the worktrees and archive PROJ-1234. All branches are merged.
negative_cases.reason_for_excluding: Post-merge teardown and archiving is feature-cleanup.

negative_cases.prompt: Review the code in order-service before I commit.
negative_cases.reason_for_excluding: Code review without a branch disposition request is code-review-request.
Test execution alone, no branch disposition intent.

negative_cases.prompt: Ship this feature — PROJ-5678, all three services are approved.
negative_cases.reason_for_excluding: Multi-service feature ship is multi-branch-merge, which will then invoke finishing-a-feature-branch per worktree.

negative_cases.prompt: What commits are in this branch?
